rows.description: from Mrs Shiers Augm. to repay ye Deo. of Money out of ye Chest to pay ye X mas
rows.notes: Amount reads as '50 00 00' clearly. Description is long but readable; a few abbreviations ('Deo.' / 'X mas') are interpreted as they appear.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Repayment/augmentation from Mrs Shiers to reimburse money taken from the chest for Christmas expenses, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.description: from Mrs Jennings to ye Rent at Day 1744
rows.notes: Pounds and shillings read clearly as '22 02'. Pence column shows '1' with a small trailing loop/mark interpreted as '3q' (three farthings = 0.75). The fraction mark is faint but consistent with 3q.
rows.english_description: Payment from Mrs Jennings toward the rent due at Lady Day (Annunciation) 1744.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Mrs Jennings toward the rent due at Lady Day (Annunciation) 1744, amounting to £22 2s 1¾d.

rows.description: from Mr Lord ½ yr & Day 1744

rows.english_description_with_amount: Quarterly payment from Mrs Roupier at Midsummer, amounting to £1 2s 6d.

rows.notes: A subtotal number is present at this location reading '210 05 02½'. The hundreds digit (2) and tens (1) are legible but a faint possibility exists it could be '240'; chosen as '210' based on the visible pen strokes. Fraction is a halfpenny (loop).
Section header centered on the page and clearly written as 'Gent. Commoners'.

rows.notes: A small standalone amount '25 10 00' appears in the right column with a preceding 'L' mark. No label on the left. The digits are legible but context is unclear (intermediate calculation), so confidence is moderate.

Another stacked total reads '240 05 02½' (halfpenny tail visible). The hundreds/tens digits are a little faint (could be 210 in a different reading) but here read as '240' given pen thickness; marked as low-medium confidence.

Third figure in the small stacked column appears as '409 10 02' (the final digits are slightly cramped and faint). Context suggests an accumulated subtotal; reading is less certain than other numeric rows.
Label 'Sum. on Receipts' is clear. Amount reads '545 05 10' cleanly and aligns with the receipts column.
Text 'from Ball. of last Acct.' is legible and amount '93 16 11' is clear. All currency fields fall within valid ranges.
rows.english_description: Receipt from Balliol College carried forward from the previous account.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt from Balliol College of the last account, amounting to £93 16s 11d.

rows.notes: Final total line 'Sum. tot. £' with amount '739 02 09' is clearly written and well aligned; high confidence in reading.
